title: CLAUDE.md — databyderek.com
text:
Personal portfolio website for a data science career. Built with Quarto, deployed to GitHub Pages at databyderek.com via the docs/ output directory on the main branch.



_quarto.yml        # Quarto project config (output-dir: docs, theme: cosmo)
index.qmd          # Home/about page (uses "trestles" about template)
projects.qmd       # Projects portfolio page
styles.css         # Custom CSS
assets/            # All images and static files (favicon, headshot, screenshots)
docs/              # Rendered output (committed to git, served by GitHub Pages)
vancouvertrees/    # Embedded Shiny app (copied to docs/ as a resource)
CNAME              # Custom domain config (databyderek.com)




Edit .qmd files (and _quarto.yml, styles.css, etc.) as requested
After completing a set of edits, always run quarto render from the project root
Never auto-commit, stage, or push — all git operations are left to the user





Do not create new .qmd pages without asking the user first
Match the existing tone and formatting of current .qmd content
All images and static files go in assets/ — never place them at the project root
Keep things minimal — don’t add unnecessary features, plugins, or complexity





Output directory: docs/ (committed to git, GitHub Pages serves from here)
Theme: cosmo (with darkly override for dark mode on the index page)
Resources directive copies vancouvertrees/ and assets/ into docs/
The CNAME file must remain at the project root for GitHub Pages custom domain
Changes to _quarto.yml require a full quarto render to take effect




quarto render
Run from the project root (/Users/derek/Projects/databyderek.com/). This regenerates everything in docs/.

title: Data by Derek
text:
Hi, I’m Derek!
I’m a data scientist and analytics professional with 6+ years in SaaS and science.
Most recently, I was creating interactive dashboards for the construction industry. In 2025, I graduated with a Master of Data Science from UBC.

title: Data by Derek
text:
Experience
SALUS | Data Analyst | Jan 2024 - Aug 2024
MotionHall | Director of Quality | Sept 2017 - Feb 2023

title: Data by Derek
text:
Education
University of British Columbia | Vancouver, BC

Master of Data Science | Sept 2024 - June 2025

Dalhousie University | Halifax, NS

B.Sc. (Hons), Neuroscience | Sept 2014 - June 2017

title: Projects
text:
Vancouver Trees Dashboard (2025)

A side project, for fun 
Dashboard for exploring Vancouver’s public trees: - Interactive map - Street View integration - Search and sort species - Heatmap of tree height by neighbourhood

🌐 Live app

Technologies used:

R
Shiny (web dashboard framework)
Docker



Automated Medical Image Segmentation for Precision Orthopedic Surgery (2025)
Capstone project for my Master of Data Science at UBC. A collaboration with the Digital Lab at the BC Children’s Hospital.
Our team developed a web dashboard and machine learning pipeline to automatically segment bones from patient CT scans and export these for 3D printing. This end-to-end solution will allow the hospital’s clinical engineers and orthopedic surgeons to more quickly and accurately plan orthopedic surgeries, increasing caseload capacity and reducing patient wait times.
A screenshot is not available for this project due to a confidentiality agreement.
Technologies used:

Python
Pandas
NumPy
nnU-Net
Plotly Dash
Docker



Patient Validation of a Motor Imagery-based Neurofeedback Paradigm (2017)

Undergraduate Honours Project for my B.Sc. in Neuroscience at Dalhousie.
Designed and implemented digital signal processing and analysis pipelines for MEG data to evaluate a motor imagery–based neurofeedback paradigm in stroke patients. Developed preprocessing, artifact rejection, and source localization workflows in Python, supporting further clinical work in patients with stroke-related upper limb dysfunction.
Technologies used:

Python
NumPy
SciPy
Matplotlib



Android App for EEG-based Neurofeedback in Stroke Recovery (2015)

Undergraduate Research Fellowship during my B.Sc. in Neuroscience at Dalhousie. A collaboration with the IWK Health Centre.
Ported the lab’s digital signal processing algorithims for live EEG data from MATLAB to Java. Developed an Android app for live, EEG-based neurofeedback during motor imagery, providing a proof-of-concept for at-home neurofeedback therapy.
Technologies used:

MATLAB
Java
Android Studio
Android SDK
